audit_items.proof_trace: Two NPD patterns exist: (1) At L223 and L248, getpassphrase() return value is passed directly to strdup() without a prior null check — if getpassphrase() returns NULL (non-interactive terminal), strdup(NULL) crashes internally before the intended null guard at L226/L251 is ever reached. (2) At L128→L135 and L143→L150, strdup(optarg) return value is used in strlen() without any null check, so OOM-induced NULL from strdup causes strlen(NULL) UB.
audit_items.attack_scenario: Run `ldappasswd -A < /dev/null` (or in any non-interactive/scripted environment): want_oldpw is set, getpassphrase() returns NULL due to no controlling terminal, strdup(NULL) dereferences NULL inside the C library, crashing the process before the intended null check at L226 is reached. The -S flag triggers the same crash via L248.
